use_case: verify egress sizing and exit count
header_note: This checklist is derived from D:/sbc_consultx/wiki/synthesis/egress-design-checklist.md and converted to ordered steps. It is NOT legal source. Each step must be paired with a verbatim citation from the cited SBC 801 Chapter 10 section.

ar: ما المساحات المستخدمة في حساب الحمولة؟
ما طول مسار السفر الأقصى الفعلي بالمتر؟
ما عرض الأبواب والسلالم والممرات بالملم؟
هل توجد ممرات مسدودة؟ ما طولها؟
هل توجد إضاءة طوارئ وعلامات مخارج مضيئة؟
en: What is the occupancy and Table 1004.5 factor?
Which floor areas feed the occupant-load calculation?
What is the longest actual egress travel distance in metres?
What is the longest common path of travel?
What are the door, stair, and corridor widths in mm?
Are there dead-end corridors? What length?
Are emergency lighting and illuminated exit signs provided?

steps.title: Determine occupancy and sprinkler status
steps.prompt: Confirm occupancy group, sub-classification, and whether automatic sprinklers are provided. These drive every subsequent threshold.

steps.title: Calculate occupant load (Section 1004)
steps.prompt: For each space, divide its area by the Table 1004.5 occupant-load factor to obtain the occupant load. Sum across spaces per floor.

steps.title: Determine number of required exits (Section 1006)
steps.prompt: Apply Section 1006 to determine number of exits or exit access doorways required: typically 2 for occupant load 1-500, 3 for 501-1000, 4 for >1000. Single-exit conditions are limited per 1006.2 / 1006.3.

checks.on_true: minimum 2 exits unless single-exit condition is satisfied

steps.title: Verify egress width (Section 1005)
steps.prompt: Compute required egress width using Section 1005 capacity factors (level components and doors vs stairways). Verify against drawings.

checks.condition: occupant_load * level_capacity_factor (per 1005.3.2) <= provided clear width of doors/level egress
occupant_load * stair_capacity_factor (per 1005.3.1) <= provided clear width of stairways

branches.effect: flag finding: increase clear width or add exits

steps.title: Verify travel distance (Section 1017)
steps.prompt: Measure the longest travel distance from the most remote point in each room/space to an exit. Compare against the Table 1017.2 limit for the occupancy and sprinkler status.

branches.effect: flag finding: add exit or add sprinklers (if Table 1017.2 sprinklered column is more permissive)

steps.title: Verify common path of travel (Section 1006.2)
steps.prompt: Measure the longest common path of travel and compare against the Section 1006.2 limit for occupancy and sprinkler status.

branches.effect: flag finding: relocate exit or restructure layout

steps.title: Verify door and stair design (Sections 1010 and 1011)
steps.prompt: Verify door clear width and height, swing direction, panic hardware where required, stair clear width, riser/tread dimensions, and stair enclosure.

checks.condition: door clear width and height meet 1010 minimums
stair clear width and riser/tread meet 1011 minimums
panic hardware required where occupant load triggers per 1010

branches.effect: flag finding for the specific failing element

steps.title: Verify exit signage and emergency lighting (Sections 1008 and 1013)
steps.prompt: Verify illuminated exit signs visible from distance per Section 1013 and emergency lighting illuminance per Section 1008.

checks.condition: exit signs continuously illuminated and visible per 1013
emergency lighting provides minimum illuminance per 1008

branches.effect: flag finding for the failing element

steps.title: Verify corridor width and dead-end length (Section 1020)
steps.prompt: Verify corridor minimum width per Section 1020.3 (varies with occupancy and occupant load) and that dead-end corridor lengths do not exceed Section 1020.5 limits.

branches.effect: flag finding for corridor width or dead-end length

steps.title: Render verdict, findings, and assumptions
steps.prompt: Aggregate compliance flags. List each finding with the cited Section verbatim from SBC 801. Restate any assumptions made due to missing inputs.

outputs.findings: ordered list of {step, condition, status, source_section}
outputs.conditional_flag: true if any input was assumed
outputs.assumptions_list: every assumption listed in plain language
outputs.missing_info_restated: restate missing-info checklist alongside the verdict

steps.citation_requirement: Always cite each Section 1004 / 1005 / 1006 / 1010 / 1011 / 1013 / 1017 / 1020 verbatim from SBC 801. Synthesis is not legal source.
